name: 北京
description: Beijing, the capital of China, is a city where ancient history meets modern dynamism. Home to the Forbidden City, Great Wall, and countless imperial treasures, it offers an unparalleled journey through 3,000 years of Chinese civilization. Today, Beijing blends traditional hutong neighborhoods with gleaming skyscrapers, world-class cuisine, and a thriving arts scene.
highlights: Great Wall - One of the Seven Wonders
Temple of Heaven - UNESCO World Heritage

climate.tips: Best visited in spring (April-May) or autumn (September-October) for pleasant weather. Summers are hot and humid, winters cold and dry. Spring often sees sandstorms. Pack layers for variable temperatures.

attractions.name: 故宫博物院
attractions.description: The former imperial palace complex, home to 24 emperors over 500 years. Contains 980 buildings and over 1 million cultural artifacts.
attractions.tips: Book tickets online in advance. Arrive early to avoid crowds. Audio guides available in multiple languages. Wear comfortable shoes.

attractions.name: 八达岭长城
attractions.description: The most visited section of the Great Wall, offering spectacular views and well-preserved fortifications.
attractions.tips: Avoid weekends if possible. Take a guided tour for better historical context. Bring water and snacks. The wall is steep - wear good walking shoes.

attractions.name: 天坛
attractions.description: A Ming dynasty religious complex where emperors prayed for good harvests. UNESCO World Heritage site with stunning circular architecture.
attractions.tips: Best visited in early morning when locals practice tai chi. The echo wall is fascinating - try whispering at different points.

attractions.name: 颐和园
attractions.description: The largest royal garden in the world, featuring the stunning Kunming Lake and Long Corridor with painted scenes.
attractions.tips: Rent a boat on the lake for a different perspective. The Long Corridor has over 14,000 paintings. Visit the tea house for authentic Chinese tea experience.

attractions.name: 天安门广场
attractions.description: The world's largest public square, surrounded by important government buildings and cultural monuments.
attractions.tips: Bring passport for security check. Visit early morning for flag-raising ceremony at sunrise.

attractions.name: 798艺术区
attractions.description: A former military factory turned into Beijing's premier contemporary art hub, featuring galleries, studios, cafes, and street art.
attractions.tips: Best on weekends when more galleries open. Combine with nearby 751 D-Park for industrial heritage. Great for souvenir photos.

attractions.name: 雍和宫
attractions.description: The largest and most well-preserved Tibetan Buddhist temple in Beijing, with stunning architecture and 18-meter Buddha statue.
attractions.tips: One of Beijing's most active temples. Arrive early to experience monk chanting. Best visited in morning for fewer crowds.

attractions.name: 北海公园
attractions.description: A historic imperial garden featuring the iconic White Dagoba, beautiful lakes, and traditional pavilions.
attractions.tips: Walk around the lake and up to the White Pagoda for classic Beijing views. Combine with nearby Houhai bar area.

attractions.name: 慕田峪长城
attractions.description: A fully restored section with 22 watchtowers, offering a less crowded alternative to Badaling with cable car access.
attractions.tips: Less crowded than Badaling. Book a tour or take Bus 916 from Dongzhimen. Bring layers as it is windy at the top.

attractions.name: 梨园剧场
attractions.description: Experience traditional Chinese opera with face-changing, martial arts, and singing at this dedicated theater.
attractions.tips: Book through hotel concierge or travel agency. Photos allowed before the show but not during. Great cultural introduction.

attractions.name: 南锣鼓巷
attractions.description: A historic hutong alley with trendy shops, street food, bars, and traditional courtyard residences.
attractions.tips: Visit in the evening for atmosphere. Explore side alleys for authentic hutong life. Try fried rice cake and sugar paintings.

restaurants.name: 新荣记
restaurants.description: Three Michelin-starred restaurant specializing in Zhejiang cuisine with exceptional seafood dishes.
dishHighlights: Steamed crab with aged Shaoxing wine

restaurants.name: 拾久
restaurants.address: 3rd Floor, 3rd Building, Sanlitun Bar St, Chaoyang District
restaurants.description: Modern Chinese fine dining with creative presentation and traditional flavors.

restaurants.name: ling-long
restaurants.address: 1-3/F, Zhao Fang Building, 8 Xinyuanli, Chaoyang District
restaurants.description: One Michelin star Taiwanese cuisine with avant-garde presentation by Chef Jason Wang.

restaurants.name: 御宝轩
restaurants.address: China World Mall, 1 Jianguomenwai Dajie, Chaoyang District
restaurants.description: Three-diamond Black Pearl Cantonese restaurant with exquisite dim sum and premium seafood.

restaurants.name: 北京厨房
restaurants.description: Popular local spot for authentic Beijing cuisine, especially duck.

restaurants.name: 全聚德
restaurants.description: Legendary Peking duck restaurant established in 1864. The definitive Beijing duck experience.

restaurants.name: 便宜坊
restaurants.description: Oldest Peking duck restaurant (established 1855) with traditional hang-roasting method.

restaurants.name: 局气
restaurants.description: Popular chain serving authentic Beijing home-style dishes in traditional atmosphere.

restaurants.name: 花家怡园
restaurants.description: Historic restaurant in a converted courtyard, known for imperial-style dishes.

restaurants.name: 利群烤鸭
restaurants.description: Famous hole-in-the-wall Peking duck restaurant favored by celebrities, no-frills authentic experience.

restaurants.name: 大董
restaurants.address: 3rd Floor, Jinbau Center, 88 Jinbao St, Dongcheng
restaurants.description: Michelin-starred restaurant revolutionizing Peking duck with modern techniques and lighter, crispier skin.

arrival.to: Beijing Capital International Airport (PEK) / Daxing International Airport (PKX)
arrival.duration: Varies by origin (8-14 hours from Europe/Americas)
arrival.tips: Daxing is newer with better facilities. PEK is closer to city center. High-speed trains from many cities.

arrival.to: Beijing Railway Stations (Beijing, Beijing West, Beijing North)
arrival.tips: High-speed trains are comfortable and punctual. Book through 12306 app or station.

metro: Single ride: 3-10 CNY depending on distance
Metro maps available in English at stations
bus: Extensive network covering areas not served by metro
Download Beijing Transport app for real-time info
taxi: Didi app works well for English speakers
bike: Unlock: 1-2 CNY, then 0.5-1 CNY per 15 minutes
Bike lanes common, follow traffic rules
International credit cards work with Alipay/WeChat

hotels.name: 北京华尔道夫酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book directly for best rates. Consider weekend packages.

hotels.name: 北京王府半岛酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Check Peninsula Academy for exclusive experiences.

hotels.name: 北京千禧大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Weekend rates often lower. Check travel sites for deals.

hotels.name: 北京希尔顿酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Hilton Honors members get better deals.

hotels.name: 北京亮马河酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Chinese travel apps often have good deals.

payment.description: Most widely accepted mobile payment in China
howToUse: Link international credit card (Visa/Mastercard accepted at many merchants)
Or open a Chinese bank account for full features
QR code payment at most merchants
tips: Many small vendors only accept Alipay/WeChat
Download before arrival as app stores may not have it
Foreign cards may not work for person-to-person transfers

payment.description: Second most popular payment method, integrated with social app
howToUse: Link international card through WeChat Pay
Some merchants prefer WeChat over Alipay
tips: Some apps require WeChat for authentication

payment.description: Still accepted everywhere, essential for markets and small vendors
howToUse: ATMs widely available (may charge fees for foreign cards)
500 CNY and 100 CNY notes most common
tips: Keep small bills for tips and small purchases
Bureau de change at airports offer poor rates
ICBC and Bank of China ATMs accept foreign cards

payment.description: Accepted at hotels, large restaurants, and international chains
howToUse: Visa and Mastercard widely accepted at upscale venues
Exchange rates applied by your bank
tips: Call your bank to enable international transactions
Notify them of travel plans to avoid fraud alerts
Some places add foreign transaction fees

culturalTips.title: Greetings & Titles
culturalTips.content: Use 'ni hao' (hello) and 'xie xie' (thank you). Address people by surname + title (e.g., Wang Xiansheng for Mr. Wang). Handshakes are common in business settings.

culturalTips.title: Dining Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Wait to be seated. Don't stick chopsticks vertically in rice - it resembles funeral rituals. Leave some food on your plate to show you've had enough. Tipping is not expected.

culturalTips.title: Business Card Exchange
culturalTips.content: Present and receive business cards with both hands. Take a moment to read the card before putting it away respectfully.

culturalTips.title: Photography Rules
culturalTips.content: Ask permission before photographing people, especially in rural areas. Some attractions prohibit photography inside. Military areas are always off-limits.

culturalTips.title: Bargaining
culturalTips.content: Bargaining is expected at markets and small shops. Start at 30-50% of the asking price. Be friendly - it's a social activity. Walk away if you can't agree.

emergencyContacts.name: 北京急救中心
emergencyContacts.notes: English operators available at some locations

emergencyContacts.name: 北京市公安局
emergencyContacts.notes: Tourist police stations at major attractions

emergencyContacts.name: 北京协和医院
emergencyContacts.notes: Best public hospital for complex cases

quickFacts.water: Tap water is boiled before drinking; bottled water widely available
